title: SACRAMENTO (January 16, 2024) – Assemblymember Diane Dixon (R-Newport Beach) introduced House Resolution 5 to raise awareness about January Human Trafficking Prevention Month.

  "claims": [
    "In California alone, there were 1,118 cases of trafficking identified and 1,910 victims involved in these cases in 2022 according to the National Human Trafficking Hotline.",
    "The impact of violence on a person should never be underestimated, and the sooner we can help victims recover from trauma, the better.",
